summary: Iteration 27 — Job Deep Search authenticity + employer verification pipeline VERIFIED end-to-end. Backend: 7/7 pytest cases pass (deep-search response shape with total_after_authenticity/total_after_employer_verify/rejection_counts/stale_purged fields, top_job.employer_verified=True, 55 verified jobs > 20 threshold, verified-feed rejects all blocklisted content-farm sources, employer-verified-only default, opt-out via require_employer_verified=false returns >= verified count, idempotent second deep-search). Backend logs confirm auth-filter rejections {blocklisted_source:15, no_location:7-13, untrusted_domain:10, inactive_url:80-91, employer_unverified:11-14}. Frontend: 55/55 job cards render the green VERIFIED pill next to employer name, Emory University cards show '201 Dowman Dr' address line as expected, and the unique source pills across the entire feed are only trusted boards (CUNY Jobs, HigherEdJobs, LinkedIn, USAJobs) — ZERO blocklisted content-farm cards. Location-type distribution is mixed (not 100% remote). All apply_url_final hosts are on the trusted whitelist or employer-own domains.

issues: Aggregate 'Sources active' header at top of page lists ALL raw scraped sources including blocklisted names (2.Halvolink, Learn4Good, SaluteMyJob, BeBee, Jobrapido, Jobright, Jobilize, Bandana.com, CazVid, Trabajo.org, DailyRemote, Learnun0n.blogspot.com, Mediabistro). These sources are correctly filtered out of the visible job cards, but showing their raw counts in the header can confuse users into thinking those content-farm cards exist in their feed. Consider filtering the counts_by_source dict returned by /api/jobs/verified-feed to exclude BLOCKLIST_SOURCES before returning, or filter client-side in jobs.tsx before rendering the 'Sources active' summary.

action_items: OPTIONAL/LOW: Filter counts_by_source in /api/jobs/verified-feed (or in jobs.tsx SourcesActive summary rendering) to exclude BLOCKLIST_SOURCES so the header does not surface content-farm names to the user. Actual job cards are already clean.
critical_code_review_comments: jobs_deep_search.py is 1274 lines — recommend splitting into modules (fetchers/, filters.py, employer.py, feed.py) when convenient; not blocking.

verified-feed aggregates counts_by_source across ALL rows in jobs_feed (including inactive/unverified), which is why blocklisted-source labels still appear in the 'Sources active' UI banner even though cards themselves are filtered.

success_rate.frontend: 100% (all Iteration 27 acceptance criteria met — VERIFIED pill on 55/55 cards, address line on Emory cards, no blocklisted card source pills)

seed_data_creation: None. Used the existing test1@plos.app account; deep-search populated jobs_feed with fresh data (55 verified cards from LinkedIn/USAJobs/HigherEdJobs/CUNY).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend deep-search takes 15-25s per call (SerpApi + link-verify + employer-verify). Reuse the deep-search response as a pytest module-scoped fixture to avoid burning SerpApi quota. Devex RSS (403) and ReliefWeb v1 API (410 Gone) both fail — these are external-source outages, not regressions; the pipeline gracefully continues. Employer-verify SerpApi google_local lookups occasionally return empty (logged as '[employer-verify] err <employer>:'); this is expected and rejected employers land in rejection_counts.employer_unverified.
rca_of_issue: N/A — no bug reproduced. Only optional UX suggestion around the 'Sources active' aggregate header.
